_notes: Quarantined test fixture. narrator_type='test' blocks all family-truth writes. Do NOT edit ad-hoc — changes invalidate baseline comparisons in regression runs.

basics.occupation: Art teacher (retired); community mural program founder

kinship.notes: Built their kitchen table from a single piece of ponderosa pine. Died 1982 after a long illness.

kinship.occupation: Seamstress; altered wedding dresses and christening gowns for neighbors
kinship.notes: Sang while she sewed. Taught Elena every song she knew. Died 1997.
Four years older. Became a folklorist at UNM.

notes: Grew up in a low adobe house with a deep front porch on Ledoux Street in Taos.
Remembers the smell of piñon smoke, her mother's bread on Saturdays, and the bells at San Francisco de Asís church.
Earned a BFA from the University of New Mexico in 1965.
Taught art in Santa Fe schools for 27 years.
Founded a community mural program in 1988 that painted over twenty murals in northern New Mexico villages.
Returned to Taos in retirement in 2004. Lives two blocks from her childhood home.
Tells stories in rolling sentences, with sensory detail, often circling back to music, kitchens, weather, and neighborhood festivals before landing on the point.
